prose:
Property: total electronic energy (single-point) and associated electrostatics of formaldehyde.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical extended tight-binding) using the xtb backend (density-fit integral approximation is intrinsic to the method).
Geometry provenance: fixed coordinates taken from user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz; no further relaxation (single-point only → GFN2-xTB//user geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: xtb via chemkit engine (version not explicitly echoed by backend; xtb standard build).
Convergence & caveats: SCF reached convergence; integrity check passed (partial charges sum to 0 within 3×10⁻¹⁶ e). No warnings were returned by the engine.
Headline results (gas phase):
• Total energy = −195.21818 eV (−7.1741356 Ha, −4501.8381 kcal mol⁻¹)
• Dipole moment = 2.4870753 D (vector [−2.4870753, 5.96×10⁻⁵, 5.79×10⁻⁵] D)
• Mulliken partial charges: O −0.278 e, C +0.174 e, H +0.052 e, H +0.052 e (sum ≈ 0 e).
Live log: the calculation streamed to an electrostatics_*.out file in the working directory (path was shown at launch and can be tailed for the full SCF transcript).
These figures are computed, not experimental; provenance = computed.
